events.title: EC archives of the week (19-25/07): Birth of Jacques Delors (20/07/2025, 101 years ago) Signing of the Yaoundé I Convention (20/07/1963, 63 years ago) G7/G8 Summit: meeting between Romano Prodi, Bob Geldof, Bono, and Lorenzo Jovanotti, artists et representatives of the "Drop the Debt" organization (20/07/2001, 25 years ago) Belgium National Day (21/07) Best-of Eurozone Summit, Brussels (21/07/2011, 15 years ago) Entry into force of the Treaty of Paris establishing the European Coal and Steel Community (ECSC) (23/07/1952, 74 years ago)

items.title: Jacques Delors, President of the European Commission (1985 - 1995)
items.summary: Jacques Delors was President of the European Commission from 1985 until 1995. He passed away on 27 December 2023. This archive video illustrates key moments and events during the ten-year mandate of Jacques Delors at the Presidency of the European Commission

items.title: Signing of the Yaoundé I Convention
items.summary: The signing of the first Convention of Association between the six Member States of the EEC and eighteen African countries and Madagascar (AASM) took place in Yaoundé (Cameroun) on 20 July 1963. Were present the Representatives of the Member States of the EEC, the Members of the Commission of the EEC and the Represenatives of the African and Malagasy Head of States.

items.title: G7/G8 Summit: meeting between Romano Prodi, Bob Geldof, Bono, and Lorenzo Jovanotti, artists et representatives of the "Drop the Debt" organization
items.summary: Romano Prodi, President of the EC, met the artists Bob Geldof, Bono, and Lorenzo Jovanotti, as representatives of the organisation "Drop the Debt"

items.title: EC archives of the week (19-25/07): Birth of Jacques Delors (20/07/2025, 101 years ago) Signing of the Yaoundé I Convention (20/07/1963, 63 years ago) G7/G8 Summit: meeting between Romano Prodi, Bob Geldof, Bono, and Lorenzo Jovanotti, artists et representatives of the "Drop the Debt" organization (20/07/2001, 25 years ago) Belgium National Day (21/07) Best-of Eurozone Summit, Brussels (21/07/2011, 15 years ago) Entry into force of the Treaty of Paris establishing the European Coal and Steel Community (ECSC) (23/07/1952, 74 years ago)
items.summary: This Infoclip presents images of emblematic places in Brussels (Belgium), such as the Belgian Federal Parliament, the Royal Palace, the Leopold Park, the Belgian Ministry of Foreign Affairs, the Monts des Arts, city centre streets, the Galeries Royales Saint-Hubert, the Grand Place and the European Quarter.

items.title: European Union Capitals: Brussels, Belgium
items.summary: This stockshot provides illustrative images of Brussels, capital of Belgium, filmed in November and December 2020. It presents general views of the city, official buildings and sights, general atmosphere in the streets and historic places and buildings in the heart of the city during the coronavirus pandemic.

items.title: Eurozone Summit, Brussels, 21/07/2011
items.summary: Euro area leaders gathered in Brussels on 21 July 2011 for an emergency summit to discuss the financial stability of the euro area and a second international bailout for Greece. The euro area countries and the International Monetary Fund gave Greece a second bailout worth 109 billion euro, on top of the 110 billion euro already granted a year before. Banks and other private investors will contribute some 37 billion euro to the rescue package by either rolling over Greek debt, swapping it for new bonds with lower interest rates or selling the bonds back to Greece at a low price. The euro area will provide some form of guarantees to the new Greek bonds rated at "selective default", so that Greek banks will be able to continue accessing liquidity support from the European Central Bank.

items.title: History of a Treaty
items.summary: The events which led to the signing of the Paris Treaty on April 18, 1951. Robert Schuman's declaration on May 9, 1950. Survey of the institutions of the Coal and Steel Community. Speeches by Jean Monnet then president of the High Authority. First results.

items.title: Retrospective on Robert Schuman from 1947 to 1963
items.summary: This stockshot presents a portrait of Robert Schuman. From the Lorraine region, Schuman was the former President of the French Council, the former French Minister for Foreign Affairs, the President of the European Parliament and its President of honour. The three European Communities owe him their existence. The famous declaration of 9 May 1950 materialized an idea of Jean Monnet, the project known as the Schuman Plan, which then became the European Coal and Steel Community (ECSC). It presents a new conception of the relations between European nations, establishing independent institutions with powers of their own. This fervent French patriot dedicated his life to two tasks: European construction and Franco-German reconciliation, its precondition.
